title: 8 Originator Information on PaymentStream AFT
body_text:
8	Originator Information on PaymentStream AFT
8.1	Overview of Originator Information on PaymentStream AFT
This Chapter provides information for financial institutions on how to update specific sections on the Originator Information page in
PaymentStreamâ¢
AFT. The Originator Information includes the following sections:
Originator ID Information
Contact Information
Limits
Originator Details
Settlement Account Information
Returns Account Information
The information in the Returns Account Information section is displayed in payor/payee records. Your financial institution can edit the Returns Account Information, but when this information is changed, only new payor/payee records include the revised information. The name of the financial institution in the Returns Account Information section is limited to branches of the same financial institution.
In the Contact Information section, a primary email address is mandatory. The primary email can be a group email. In addition, up to 30 email addresses can be added in the Additional Emails field. The system sends emails to the Originatorâs primary email address and any addresses entered in the Additional Emails field to notify of manually and/or automatically released AFT files.
8.2	Updating Contact Information, Emails, and Limits for Originators
Authorized financial institution users can update contact information, email addresses, and limits on
PaymentStream
AFT for their Originators and for their business membersâ Originators.
To update Originator information, authorized users proceed as follows:
Log in to
PaymentStream
AFT as described in Section 7.2, Logging in to PaymentStream AFT â Financial Institution Users.
Select the appropriate Originator ID.
Select
Originator Information
and select
Submit
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Selecting the Originator ID and the Action
Select the applicable field in the Contact Information and/or Limits section.
Enter the new information.
Select
Update
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Updating Originator Information
8.3	Requesting Originator Information Changes on PaymentStream AFT
This Section applies to financial institutions that want to change Originator information other than contact information and limits.
Note:	Authorized users from your financial institution can update contact information, email addresses, and limits on
PaymentStream
AFT for your Originators and for your business membersâ Originators. See Section 8.2, Updating Contact Information, Emails, and Limits for Originators.
Proceed as follows to request an update to additional Originator information on
PaymentStream
AFT:
Complete the
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application, Form 5329. See Figure 3. Completion of this form is self-explanatory.
Figure 3: PaymentStream AFT Service Application, Form 5329
Ensure that the form is authorized by two signing officers on file at Central 1.
Forward the form to Central 1 as indicated on the form.
If you modify any of the following default information, the changes are applied to new payor/payee records only:
Return account number, route, and transit number. The ID and bank name associated with returns canât be modified.
Frequency
Transaction type
Existing payor/payee records wonât be automatically changed. Itâs the Originatorâs responsibility to ensure that changes are made to existing payor/payee records when default information is changed.
Related Information
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application,
Form 5329
END
